title: Today's AI Brief: Our views on AI policy and political advocacy
summary: OpenAI's approach to AI policy and political advocacy

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I'm looking at a recent post from OpenAI on their views on AI policy and political advocacy. It's a timely topic, given the rapid advancements in AI and its growing impact on society. I think it's great that OpenAI is speaking up on this - it's not every day that a major AI player shares its thoughts on how to shape the future of AI.

First up, OpenAI is emphasizing transparency in their approach to AI policy and political advocacy. They're making it clear that they support thoughtful regulation and AI safety. No outside political group speaks on the company's behalf, which is an interesting point in today's world of AI development. I'm curious to see how this plays out - will other companies follow suit?

Next, I'd like to highlight OpenAI's commitment to AI safety. They're focusing on developing AI that benefits society, and they acknowledge the importance of trust in AI systems. It's a bit like building a house - you want to make sure it's sturdy before you invite people in. I'm heartened to see AI leaders prioritizing safety and responsibility.

Also on the board, OpenAI is prioritizing data boundaries. They're recognizing that data is a critical component of AI development, and they're taking steps to ensure that data is used responsibly. It's a complex issue, but it's essential for building trust in AI systems. I appreciate that OpenAI is tackling this head-on.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: Our views on AI policy and political advocacy
